description: 55M ankle dislocation with obvious deformity — Ottawa Ankle moot, X-ray regardless
transcript:
Dr. Banks: What happened?
Patient: I fell off the back of my truck. About four feet. Landed on my ankle.
Dr. Banks: Let me look. Significant deformity of the right ankle. The foot is angulated laterally. Skin is intact, no open wound.
Dr. Banks: Can you feel me touching your toes?
Patient: Yes.
Dr. Banks: Wiggle them for me? Good. Capillary refill is brisk. Dorsalis pedis is palpable. Foot is warm and pink.
Dr. Banks: We need to reduce this and get an X-ray. Let me get fentanyl, prep for procedural sedation. We're going to reduce it under sedation, splint, and get pre- and post-reduction X-rays.
Dr. Banks: Nurse, page ortho, page anesthesia for backup if we need it. Set up the conscious sedation kit.

groundTruth.notes: Ottawa Ankle is a rule-out for whether to image a low-suspicion ankle injury. With obvious deformity (likely fracture-dislocation), the patient is getting imaging regardless — the rule is moot. Doctor is in management mode (reduction, sedation, ortho consult), not deciding on imaging. Surfacing the rule would be noise.
